title: T-009 — report_access_gate_v1 DSL rule + middleware enforcement (12-REPORTING §7.1, §11 V-RPT-ACCESS-*)
prompt:
# T-009 — report_access_gate_v1 DSL rule + middleware

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §7.1 (rule spec), §11 V-RPT-ACCESS-1..5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register `report_access_gate_v1` DSL rule in 02-SETTINGS §7 rule registry (P1 active). Implement Next.js `middleware.ts` segment for `/reporting/*` that evaluates the rule before any route renders: (1) session present, (2) user_role permitted for dashboard_id, (3) org_id from session matches request scope, (4) site_id ∈ user.accessible_sites, (5) feature_flag eval. Deny → audit `report_access_audits` row + 403 redirect to /403; allow → continue.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0086_reporting_dsl_rule_seed.sql` — inserts `report_access_gate_v1` row into 02-SETTINGS rules registry table (`dsl_rules` or equivalent — verify current schema before SQL is written; do NOT assume).
2. Rule evaluator `packages/reporting/src/rules/report-access-gate.ts` implements V-RPT-ACCESS-1..5; pure function `(ctx: {userId, role, dashboardId, orgId, siteId, requestOrgId, requestSiteId, accessibleSites}) => {allow: boolean, denyReason?: string}`.
3. Middleware `apps/web/middleware.ts` (extend existing) — for `/reporting/*` paths: load session, call rule evaluator, on deny → write report_access_audits row (T-004) + redirect to /403; on allow → continue.
4. Server Action wrapper `withReportingAccess(dashboardId, action)` for inline action checks (e.g., from RPT-020 access_denied_modal path); writes inline_denied audit on deny.
5. Vitest contract test: (a) V-RPT-ACCESS-1 (no session → 401), (b) V-RPT-ACCESS-2 (wrong role → 403 + audit row), (c) V-RPT-ACCESS-3 (cross-org → 403 + audit), (d) V-RPT-ACCESS-4 (site not in accessible_sites → 403), (e) V-RPT-ACCESS-5 (flag-off dashboard → 404), (f) allow path writes allow row to report_access_audits.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0086_reporting_dsl_rule_seed.sql [create]
- packages/reporting/src/rules/report-access-gate.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/src/rules/__tests__/report-access-gate.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/middleware.ts [modify]
- apps/web/__tests__/middleware-reporting.test.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/src/with-reporting-access.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0086 runs, when `dsl_rules` is queried for `report_access_gate_v1`, then exactly one P1-active row exists with input/output schema matching PRD §7.1.
2. Given a request to /reporting/factory-overview without session, when middleware runs, then it returns 401 (V-RPT-ACCESS-1) and writes a report_access_audits row with result='deny' + deny_reason='unauthenticated'.
3. Given a user with role reporting_viewer requests /reporting/integration-health (admin-only), when middleware runs, then it returns 403 + audit row with deny_reason='role_not_permitted' (V-RPT-ACCESS-2).
4. Given user session org_id=A and request scope tries org_id=B, when middleware runs, then 403 + audit (V-RPT-ACCESS-3 cross-org).
5. Given dashboard `factory-overview-p2` has feature_flag='reporting.v2_dashboards' OFF for the org, when request hits, then 404 (V-RPT-ACCESS-5, hide existence).
6. Given an allow path completes, when audit table is checked, then a row with result='allow' is written + the page renders.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: `packages/reporting/src/rules/__tests__/report-access-gate.test.ts` covers V-RPT-ACCESS-1..5 with pure-function inputs; `apps/web/__tests__/middleware-reporting.test.ts` covers end-to-end middleware behaviour with mocked sessions.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/reporting test report-access-gate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test middleware-reporting`.
- GREEN: write rule + middleware + Server Action wrapper.

## Risk red lines
- Do not gate at component level only — middleware path-level enforcement per RPT-009/RPT-010/RPT-020 translation-notes (admin-only routes); rely on session in Server Component for inline UI.
- Do not log raw user_role to audit table — only allow/deny + deny_reason enum.
- Do not skip ip_address/user_agent capture (forensics).
- Do not let V-RPT-ACCESS-5 leak existence — return 404 not 403 when flag-off (hide).
- Do not write the rule evaluator with side effects — pure function; audit write happens in middleware, not in evaluator.
- Do not couple rule evaluation to specific Permission enum strings — read dashboards_catalog.required_role and compare against the user's role set per RBAC mapping (T-001 enum gives the strings, the rule checks the join).
- Do not block deferred PRD §16 anchor lookup — verify the rule registry table name from migration history before writing INSERT (see kira_hq_red_prompt_verify_schema memory entry).
